pr.title: fix(snapserver): 2 MiB response cap + 4s per-request time budget (#1145)
pr.body:
* fix(snapserver): 2 MiB response cap; 4s per-request time budget

Cap GetAccountRange, GetStorageRanges, GetByteCodes, and GetTrieNodes
responses at 2 MiB. The SNAP spec (EIP-2124) permits servers to truncate
responses at a soft limit; without a cap, large trie responses caused
memory spikes and exceeded the RLPx frame size, causing peer disconnects.

Add a 4-second per-request time budget to GetTrieNodes. Trie node lookups
traverse RocksDB non-linearly; a malformed or overly broad request can
hold the serving goroutine indefinitely. The budget applies per request,
not per node, allowing legitimate large requests while bounding worst-case
latency.

Both limits match the go-ethereum snap server implementation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* style: scalafmtAll on SnapServer.scala (CI fix)

Unblocks Compile/scalafmtCheck. Formatting-only change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
